Show me an example of string/normalize in Sema.

;; NFC: combine decomposed characters
;; e + combining acute accent → é
(string/normalize "e\u0301" :nfc)    ; => "é"

;; NFD: decompose composed characters
(string/length (string/normalize "é" :nfd))  ; => 2 (e + combining accent)

;; NFKC/NFKD: compatibility decomposition (ligatures, etc.)
(string/normalize "\uFB01" :nfkc)    ; => "fi" (ﬁ ligature → two letters)

;; String form names also work
(string/normalize "e\u0301" "NFC")   ; => "é"

Write Sema code: Async tasks run cooperatively on the VM scheduler. `(async expr)` spawns a task; `(await p)` waits for its result. The output is the SAME every run — that's intentional, not a bug. The scheduler is deterministic (ready tasks resume in spawn order), so there's no random reordering. Note also that in the browser playground `async/sleep` returns immediately (it can't block the UI thread), so equal-sleep tasks like these always finish in spawn order. See worker-pool.sema for visible round-robin interleaving. pretend this is real work

(define (slow-square n)
  (async/sleep 50)        
  (* n n))

Write a Sema expression: auto gensym 1000 unique

(begin
          (define syms (map (fn (_) (symbol->string (gensym "s"))) (range 1000)))
          (define sorted (sort syms))
          (define (has-dup? lst)
            (if (or (null? lst) (null? (cdr lst)))
              #f
              (if (= (car lst) (cadr lst))
                #t
                (has-dup? (cdr lst)))))
          (not (has-dup? sorted)))

Write Sema code: force the thunk Take n elements from a stream, return as a list

(define (stream-take n s)
  (let loop ((i n) (s s) (acc '()))
    (if (or (= i 0) (stream-empty? s))
      (reverse acc)
      (loop (- i 1) (stream-cdr s) (cons (stream-car s) acc)))))

Write Sema code: Map over a stream

(define (stream-map f s)
  (if (stream-empty? s)
    stream-empty
    (stream-cons (f (stream-car s))
      (fn () (stream-map f (stream-cdr s))))))

Write Sema code for: style code

(defmacro some-> (val . forms)
  (if (null? forms) val
    (let ((form (car forms)) (rest (cdr forms)))
      (if (list? form)
        `(let ((__v ,val))
           (if (nil? __v) nil (some-> (,(car form) __v ,@(cdr form)) ,@rest)))
        `(let ((__v ,val))
           (if (nil? __v) nil (some-> (,form __v) ,@rest)))))))
